title: Induction Forge Temperature Controller
description: PID temperature controller for a DIY IGBT induction heater coil used as a small metal forge (for knife-making, blacksmithing, or heat-treatment). A PT100 RTD probe near the workpiece feeds a MAX31865 SPI amplifier; the PID loop drives a PWM MOSFET gate that modulates the induction heater's power IGBT driver enable input. Setpoint and live temperature shown on OLED; rotary encoder adjusts setpoint.
objective: Regulate forge temperature 200–1200 °C within ±15 °C of setpoint; power modulation 0–100 % via PWM to induction driver; manual setpoint control; auto-cutoff above 1250 °C safety limit.
constraints: 230 V / 2 kW induction heater module (ZVS driver); 12 V DC auxiliary supply for controller logic; safety interlock (thermal cutoff relay); ≤ £40 controller BOM.
supporting_fact_titles: Induction Heating — IGBT Resonant Series Circuit
N-Channel MOSFET as a Low-Side Power Switch
Rotary Encoder — Incremental A/B Quadrature Pulse Counting
OLED SSD1306 Monochrome Display I²C Interface

element_usage_notes.IGBT Resonant Induction Heating Circuit: ZVS induction heater module (2 kW, 12–48 V input); WORK_EN gate exposed; controller Arduino GPIO drives N-MOSFET that controls WORK_EN via open-drain — HIGH = heater enabled.
element_usage_notes.MAX31865 PT100 RTD Amplifier: Pt100 in ceramic sheath, 200 mm stainless probe; SPI CS=D10; 2-wire; polled at 10 Hz; Adafruit MAX31865 library (Callendar-Van Dusen coefficient A, B).
element_usage_notes.N-Channel MOSFET Low-Side Load Switch: IRLZ44N low-side switch on induction driver WORK_EN pin; 10 Hz PWM from D5 modulates heating duty; gate via 100 Ω to Nano D5; drain to WORK_EN, source to GND.
element_usage_notes.Rotary Encoder User-Interface Navigation: KY-040; CLK D2 INT0; 10 °C per detent; long-press activates/deactivates forge; safety cutoff disables independently of encoder.
element_usage_notes.SSD1306 OLED Display UI Module: 128×64; setpoint °C (large) / actual °C / PID power % / status (HEATING/AT TEMP/CUTOFF).
element_usage_notes.Arduino Uno/Nano MCU Platform: Nano 3.0; SPI MAX31865 D10–D13; I²C OLED A4/A5; MOSFET PWM D5; safety relay D8; buzzer D9.

element_interactions.data: temp_c (float, range −200 to 850 °C) via SPI at 10 Hz
element_interactions.description: 10-Hz SPI read; 5-sample median filter removes noise from induction EMI. Temperature fed to PID as process variable. Values > 1250 °C trigger immediate safety cutoff.
CW = setpoint +10 °C; CCW = setpoint −10 °C; range 200–1200 °C. Long press (> 1 s) toggles forge active state. Short press with forge off = no action.

element_interactions.data: 10 Hz PWM duty (0–100 %) on GPIO D5
element_interactions.description: PID output mapped to 100 ms window duty cycle on D5. MOSFET drain pulls WORK_EN LOW when gate = LOW (heater off), HIGH when gate = HIGH (heater on). Safety cutoff forces D5 LOW and D8 (relay) LOW.

element_interactions.data: I²C update (setpoint, actual, pid_pct, status_str) at 2 Hz
element_interactions.description: Status: HEATING if T < SP−30 °C; AT TEMP if |T−SP| ≤ 15 °C; CUTOFF if T > 1250 °C. Flashes CUTOFF in inverted text with buzzer.

element_interactions.data: WORK_EN signal (3.3–5 V logic, active HIGH to enable heater)
element_interactions.description: Induction driver WORK_EN pin connected to MOSFET drain via pull-up to 5 V. MOSFET pulls LOW to disable; floating (MOSFET off) = heater enabled by pull-up.
